company_snapshot.current_search_vendor: Oracle Endeca (REMOVED) — no confirmed active replacement

executives.name: Philip B. Daniele III
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: All while continuing to invest in technology to improve customer service and our AutoZoners' ability to deliver on our promise of Wow customer service.
executives.relevance: CEO commits to technology investment as a board-level priority — exact mandate Algolia search fulfills at the customer touchpoint

executives.name: Philip B. Daniele III
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: This year, we are investing nearly $1.6 billion in CapEx to drive our strategic growth priorities, and we expect to invest a similar amount next year.
executives.relevance: At $1.6B capex, a $750K Algolia investment is 0.05% of annual capex — budget is not the constraint; the conversation is strategic

executives.name: Philip B. Daniele III
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: We are in the final stages of our Supply Chain 2030 project, which began in 2019.
executives.relevance: Supply chain modernization completing — digital catalog and search infrastructure is the logical next modernization chapter

executives.name: Philip B. Daniele III
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: The majority of these investments will be in accelerated store growth, including hubs and Mega Hubs, placing more inventory closer to our customers.
executives.relevance: Mega Hub SKU explosion (80K–110K SKUs per location, target 300+ hubs) means search relevance problem compounds as catalog scale grows

executives.name: Philip B. Daniele III
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: Our commercial sales results continue to be driven by our improved satellite store inventory availability, significant improvements in hub and Mega Hub coverage, the continued strength of our Duralast brand, and high levels of execution on our initiatives to improve speed of delivery and customer service.
executives.relevance: CEO cites Duralast brand strength — yet Phase 2 audit found Duralast brand search returns only 2 results. Direct contradiction = high-impact gap pair.

executives.name: Philip B. Daniele III
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: We will continue investing in technology to improve customer service and our AutoZoners' ability to deliver on our promise of 'Wow Customer Service.'
executives.relevance: Technology investment language is consistent across multiple quarters — standing CEO priority, not a one-time mention

executives.name: Jamere Jackson
executives.title: EVP & CFO
executives.quote: Our commercial acceleration initiatives continue to deliver strong results as we grow share by winning new business and increasing our share of wallet with existing customers.
executives.relevance: DIFM commercial at +9.8% SSS is the highest-growth segment — and the segment most dependent on catalog search accuracy for parts ordering

executives.name: Jamere Jackson
executives.title: EVP & CFO
executives.quote: The expansion of coverage and parts availability continues to deliver a meaningful sales lift to both our commercial and DIY business.
executives.relevance: Parts coverage expansion only delivers sales lift if customers can find those new parts — search infrastructure must scale with catalog growth

executives.name: Jamere Jackson
executives.title: EVP & CFO
executives.quote: These investments will also pay dividends in customer experience, speed of delivery, and productivity, all of which will help us grow market share.
executives.relevance: CFO frames customer experience as the ROI of technology investment — Algolia's primary value vector, measurable in conversion and revenue

executives.name: AutoZone Corporate
executives.title: 10-K FY2025, Item 7 MD&A
executives.quote: AutoZone continues to invest in its information systems infrastructure at increasing levels, as these investments will be core to its future customer service offerings.
executives.relevance: Board-disclosed technology budget at 'increasing levels' — confirms budget is growing and the CFO conversation is strategic, not budget-constrained

executives.name: AutoZone Corporate
executives.title: 10-K FY2025, Item 7 MD&A
executives.quote: We expect to rapidly add parts coverage to our online catalog in FY26.
executives.relevance: Catalog expansion is a stated FY2026 strategic goal — more SKUs without better search = worse findability. Algolia indexing scales instantly.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO Philip Daniele publicly committed to 'investing in technology to improve customer service' in Q2 FY2026, Q4 FY2025, and Q1 FY2026 earnings calls — three consecutive quarters.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Philip B. Daniele III, CEO — Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call
intelligence_signals.relevance: Repeated CEO commitment creates board-level mandate and budget authorization for technology vendors including search

intelligence_signals.text: AutoZone is investing nearly $1.6B in CapEx for FY2026, with technology explicitly named alongside stores and distribution centers as a capex priority.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Philip B. Daniele III, CEO — Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call
intelligence_signals.relevance: At $1.6B capex, Algolia at ~$750K/year is budget-immaterial — the conversation is strategic, not financial

intelligence_signals.text: Supply Chain 2030 project is in its 'final stages' — completing a 7-year infrastructure transformation that opens the door for the next modernization chapter: digital catalog and search.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Philip B. Daniele III, CEO — Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call
intelligence_signals.relevance: Natural transition: supply chain done → digital-to-customer layer (search) is next. Buying window is open.

intelligence_signals.text: Oracle Endeca (search) and Oracle Commerce / ATG (ecommerce platform) have both been REMOVED. Certona (recommendations engine) also REMOVED. Three major vendor slots are empty simultaneously.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Oracle stack purge creates three simultaneous replacement opportunities: search (Endeca), recommendations (Certona), and broader discovery platform

intelligence_signals.text: Lytics CDP installed January 2026 — AutoZone is actively building first-party data infrastructure, the foundation required for search personalization.
intelligence_signals.relevance: CDP adoption signals personalization roadmap is active — Algolia Personalization is the natural next layer above the CDP

intelligence_signals.text: SAP Hybris on-premise EOL is July 31, 2026 — NAPA Auto Parts (top competitor with 38.4% bounce rate and best-in-class engagement) will be mid-platform migration throughout 2026.
intelligence_signals.relevance: AutoZone can establish AI-powered search advantage before NAPA completes re-platform — first-mover window in the vertical

intelligence_signals.text: ChatGPT is AutoZone's #3 referral source, driving 316,302 visits/month — AI-referred traffic arrives with conversational, semantic queries that AutoZone's current NLP-blind search cannot handle.
intelligence_signals.relevance: AutoZone is winning AI-driven discovery but losing conversion at on-site search. NeuralSearch closes the loop.

intelligence_signals.text: Advance Auto Parts appointed new CTO Shweta Bhatia in January 2025 with explicit digital transformation mandate — running LucidWorks/Solr on HCL Commerce (dual legacy). If they modernize first, their 47.6% bounce rate closes vs. AutoZone.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Urgency: Advance Auto is feeling the search pain (47.6% bounce) and has put a CTO in place to fix it. AutoZone can leapfrog now.

intelligence_signals.text: Zero of the top 5 competitors (O'Reilly, Advance, NAPA, CarParts, RockAuto) use Algolia. All run legacy stacks: Oracle ATG, LucidWorks/Solr, SAP Hybris, Oracle Endeca (dead), or custom catalog. First-mover advantage is fully open.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Pure offensive play — no defensive urgency from Algolia customer competition, no imitation barrier. AutoZone can set the ceiling for what auto parts search can be.

intelligence_signals.text: AutoZone is actively hiring a Lead Software Engineer (eCommerce / Search) in Memphis, TN. Job posting states: 'AutoZone.com is going through big transformation, and we're investing deeply to bring new capabilities online for our customers.'
intelligence_signals.relevance: Active search hiring = they feel the pain. 'Big transformation' language = budget allocated. Algolia as 'build better with fewer hires' message is timely.

intelligence_signals.text: 10-K FY2025 discloses legacy system replacement as a named risk: 'the need to replace existing systems, some of which are older legacy systems, with successor systems.'
intelligence_signals.relevance: AutoZone's own board filing names legacy system risk. Algolia is the lowest-risk search replacement: SaaS, cloud-native, in production in 8–12 weeks.

intelligence_signals.text: 10-K FY2025: 'We expect to rapidly add parts coverage to our online catalog in FY26.' — Catalog expansion with unchanged search infrastructure makes findability worse, not better.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Catalog expansion creates urgency: new parts become invisible in search without better synonym mapping and NLP. Algolia indexing scales instantly.

competitors.name: O'Reilly Auto Parts
competitors.notes: Bounce rate 40.5%. #1 competitor by both SimilarWeb affinity and keyword overlap (61%). Oracle ATG: rigid, no NLP, no ML ranking. Detection: BuiltWith confirmed.

competitors.name: Advance Auto Parts
competitors.notes: Bounce rate 47.6% — highest in the competitive set. New CTO Jan 2025 with digital transformation mandate. HCL Commerce + LucidWorks = dual legacy. Detection: BuiltWith confirmed.

competitors.name: NAPA Auto Parts
competitors.notes: Bounce rate 38.4% — lowest in set. SAP Hybris EOL July 2026 forces migration. Best engagement metrics despite no AI search. Detection: BuiltWith confirmed.

competitors.name: CarParts.com
competitors.notes: Bounce rate 52.4% — worst in the competitive set. Pure-play ecommerce with no stores; search is their entire UX. Endeca is dead-end platform. Detection: BuiltWith confirmed.

competitors.name: RockAuto
competitors.notes: Bounce rate 38.1%. Zero keyword/NLP search — structured tree navigation only. 8.10 pages/visit is artifact of drill-down model, not search quality. Detection: BuiltWith (no platform detected).

findings.title: Typo Tolerance Failure
findings.expected_behavior: Autocorrect to 'brake pads' and return 500+ relevant results with a 'Did you mean: brake pads?' prompt
findings.actual_behavior: Typos bypass brand redirects. No 'Did you mean' functionality detected. Misspelled queries return degraded or irrelevant results.
findings.prospect_description: When a customer types 'brak pads' instead of 'brake pads', the search fails — no autocorrect, no helpful prompt, no forgiving results. The customer bounces. Google already corrects 'autzone' to 'AutoZone' (33K monthly clicks) — your on-site search should too.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia built-in typo tolerance handles 1–2 character errors natively, no configuration required. Typo-corrected results are returned in under 50ms with an optional 'Did you mean' prompt.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 12% conversion lift, -65% no-results rate after deploying Algolia for 1.5M auto parts SKU catalog

findings.title: No Query Suggestions / Empty State Gap
findings.expected_behavior: Display 5–8 popular or trending searches (e.g. 'brake pads', 'motor oil', 'battery') with category context on empty state
findings.actual_behavior: Empty search state shows no popular searches, no trending queries, no recent search history. Search bar is blank with no guidance.
findings.prospect_description: 18.9% of AutoZone's organic traffic lands on the homepage and immediately searches. An empty search box with no suggestions forces customers to know exactly what to type — missing the discovery opportunity for browsing customers.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions builds a real-time index of popular, trending, and category-specific searches. Deployed in the empty state, it guides customers toward high-converting query patterns.

findings.title: No Federated Search — Content Invisible
findings.expected_behavior: Search returns both product results AND relevant help/policy content — return policy page, store hours, how-to guides — in a unified federated results page
findings.actual_behavior: Searching 'return policy' returns product results only. Help content, policy pages, and how-to guides are siloed outside search entirely. Non-product queries show product-only results.
findings.prospect_description: A customer asking about the return policy via search sees brake pads. A first-time buyer looking for help articles sees floor mats. Content that should build trust and answer pre-purchase questions is invisible in search — a conversion killer.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Federated Search unifies product catalog, help content, FAQs, store locator, and how-to guides into a single, tabbed search results page. Customers find everything from one search bar.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Unified product + content search reduces support tickets and improves first-time buyer conversion

findings.title: No Product Recommendations — Certona Removed
findings.expected_behavior: Product detail pages show 'Frequently Bought Together', 'Customers Also Viewed', and related accessories — cross-sell and upsell driven by co-purchase signals
findings.actual_behavior: No 'Frequently Bought Together' detected on any product page. No 'Customers Also Viewed' functionality. Certona recommendations engine REMOVED (confirmed BuiltWith + SimilarWeb) with no active replacement detected.
findings.prospect_description: AutoZone removed Certona — their product recommendations engine — and has no active replacement. At $18.9B revenue with 100,000+ SKUs per Mega Hub, customers buying brake pads see nothing about rotors, brake fluid, or installation tools. The cross-sell revenue opportunity is entirely missing.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend provides FBT, 'Related Products', and 'Trending Items' models trained on AutoZone's co-purchase and co-view data. Direct replacement for Certona with better ML ranking.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 12% conversion lift after deploying Algolia recommendations for 1.5M auto parts SKU catalog

findings.title: No NLP / Semantic Search
findings.tested_query: what oil does my 2019 camry take
findings.expected_behavior: Natural language query parsed semantically — return motor oil products filtered to 2019 Toyota Camry compatibility with correct viscosity spec
findings.actual_behavior: Pure keyword matching only. Conversational or natural language queries return irrelevant or zero useful results. Vehicle lookup requires manual Fix Finder navigation — no inline semantic understanding.
findings.prospect_description: 316,000 monthly visitors arrive from ChatGPT having asked natural-language automotive questions. When they search those same questions on AutoZone.com, the search engine goes blank. The AI discovery advantage is lost at the conversion moment.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch understands natural language queries using vector search — 'what oil does my 2019 Camry take' returns 0W-20 motor oil filtered by vehicle compatibility, not keyword-matched chaos.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 30% more relevant results on natural language and long-tail queries

findings.title: Synonym Gap — 23x Result Variance
findings.expected_behavior: 'Headlamp' and 'headlight' return identical or equivalent results — unified synonym mapping ensures customers find the same 538 products regardless of which term they use
findings.actual_behavior: Searching 'headlamp' returns 23 results. Searching 'headlight' returns 538 results. A 23x variance for synonymous automotive terms. Separate category pages exist rather than unified synonym results.
findings.impact_stat: 23 results for 'headlamp' vs. 538 results for 'headlight' — 23x synonym gap confirmed in Phase 2 audit
findings.prospect_description: A customer looking for a 'headlamp' sees 23 products. The same customer who types 'headlight' sees 538. Both mean the same thing. Customers using the 'wrong' synonym find a broken catalog — and leave for O'Reilly.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Synonyms allows two-way synonym mapping: 'headlamp' ↔ 'headlight', 'brake pad' ↔ 'brake shoe', 'engine oil' ↔ 'motor oil'. Managed via no-code dashboard with AI synonym suggestions.

findings.title: Weak Brand Intent Detection — Duralast Returns 2 Results
findings.expected_behavior: Brand query 'Duralast' triggers a brand landing page or filters results to show all Duralast products (AutoZone's own private-label brand) across all categories
findings.actual_behavior: Duralast brand search returns only 2 results. AutoZone's own private-label brand — cited by the CEO in Q2 FY2026 as a commercial growth driver — is nearly invisible in on-site search.
findings.impact_stat: 2 search results for 'Duralast' — AutoZone's flagship private-label brand carrying 100,000+ SKUs
findings.prospect_description: Duralast is AutoZone's highest-margin private label — the CEO credits it as a commercial sales driver. Typing 'Duralast' into autozone.com search returns 2 products. This is not a search gap; it is a brand integrity failure.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules allow brand intent detection: a 'Duralast' query triggers a redirect rule to the Duralast brand landing page, OR pins all Duralast SKUs to the top of results with category-filtered facets.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Brand queries resolved via no-code redirect rules with zero engineering tickets

findings.title: Static Facets / No Behavioral Personalization
findings.expected_behavior: Facets adapt dynamically to battery category (Cold Cranking Amps, Reserve Capacity, Group Size, vehicle compatibility). Browsing history surfaces battery accessories or previously viewed brands.
findings.actual_behavior: Static filters (brand dropdown, vehicle fitment, generic category). Vehicle YMM persists session to session but no browse-based or purchase-history personalization detected. No facets adapting to category context.
findings.prospect_description: A customer searching 'battery' for a diesel truck needs Cold Cranking Amps and Group Size filters — not the same facets as someone searching 'wiper blades'. Static facets serve neither customer well.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Dynamic Faceting automatically orders and surfaces the most relevant filters for each query — CCA for batteries, viscosity for motor oil, size for wiper blades. Algolia Personalization layers in behavioral ranking from Lytics CDP signals.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Personalized results improve conversion 15–20% per Forrester benchmark

findings.title: No Search Intelligence Signals
findings.expected_behavior: Results page displays trending badges, bestseller labels, 'most searched this week' indicators, and click analytics-driven ranking boosts for popular products
findings.actual_behavior: No trending badges, no bestseller labels, no 'most searched' signals visible on any search results page. Products are ranked without visible behavioral intelligence signals.
findings.prospect_description: A customer searching 'brake pads' sees 100+ results with no signal of what other customers are buying. No 'bestseller' badge, no 'trending this week', no social proof in search results — reducing confidence in product selection.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Analytics and Search Insights surface trending queries, zero-result rates, click position analytics, and bestseller signals that drive result ranking. A/B testing validates relevance improvements.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Click analytics and A/B testing continuously improve search relevance and conversion rates

findings.title: Merchandising Consistency Unverified
findings.expected_behavior: Products shown in search results match those shown in browse/category navigation with consistent sorting, boosts, and promotional positioning
findings.actual_behavior: Unable to confirm search vs. browse parity in Phase 2 audit. Separate URL-based category pages for headlight vs. headlamp suggest separate content taxonomies rather than unified search-driven results.
findings.prospect_description: When search results and category browse pages show different products in different orders, customers who arrive via different entry points have inconsistent experiences — eroding trust and brand coherence.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules and Merchandising Dashboard enable no-code configuration of boosts, pins, and promotional placements that apply consistently across search and browse — ensuring a unified customer experience.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: No-code merchandising dashboard allows search and browse consistency without engineering tickets

findings.title: Acceptable Latency — Dual CDN
findings.actual_behavior: Site loads within acceptable range. Akamai Edge + Cloudflare dual CDN layer provides strong global delivery performance.
findings.prospect_description: AutoZone's infrastructure is well-optimized for speed. Akamai + Cloudflare dual-CDN delivers competitive page load times.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia guarantees sub-50ms search response via globally distributed infrastructure — improving the search latency well below the current page-level CDN baseline.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 99.999% SLA uptime with sub-50ms global search response

findings.title: Vehicle Fitment (Fix Finder) — Core Strength
findings.expected_behavior: Year/Make/Model selection filters results to compatible parts only
findings.actual_behavior: Fix Finder service provides strong Year/Make/Model filtering. Vehicle selection persists across session. Core automotive UX competency is present.
findings.prospect_description: AutoZone's Fix Finder YMM filtering is a genuine strength — customers can identify their vehicle and get fitment-filtered results. This is the right foundation to build on.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia's Intelligent Auto Parts Solution extends Fix Finder-style fitment into every search query — NLP queries like 'battery for 2019 Ram 1500' trigger automatic YMM filtering without requiring the Fix Finder tool.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Native Year/Make/Model fitment awareness built into search — purpose-built for auto parts retail

findings.title: Strong Mobile Presence — 66% of Sales on Mobile
findings.expected_behavior: Mobile search experience is responsive, fast, and search-first
findings.actual_behavior: 66% of AutoZone sales finalized on mobile devices (July 2025). Mobile app available with quick part finding. Location pages are 91–99.9% mobile traffic. Strong mobile foundation.
findings.prospect_description: AutoZone's mobile-first customer base is a strength — the opportunity is layering AI search intelligence onto that mobile foundation to convert more of the 31.3M monthly visits.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia InstantSearch is mobile-optimized by default — voice search support, mobile-responsive SAYT dropdowns, and instant filtering without page reloads. Complements the existing mobile app architecture.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Mobile-optimized InstantSearch with voice search and responsive UI components

buying_committee.name: Bailey Childress
buying_committee.title: SVP, Omnichannel & Merchandising Support
buying_committee.message: Appointed December 2024 — actively defining omnichannel roadmap. Owns BOPIS and digital merchandising where search relevance directly drives conversion.

buying_committee.name: Manoj Koratty
buying_committee.title: CTO, Customer Satisfaction
buying_committee.message: Enterprise architect background (AZO since 2013, CTO since 2018). Oracle stack migration creates open architecture decisions. Algolia's API-first model is his language.

buying_committee.name: K. Michelle Borninkhof
buying_committee.title: SVP & CIO, Customer Satisfaction
buying_committee.message: Hired from McDonald's/Walmart (2021) — has operated enterprise-scale search at comparable volume. Controls IT infrastructure budget. Natural entry point via McDonald's/Walmart Algolia familiarity.

buying_committee.name: Jamey Maki
buying_committee.title: VP, eCommerce & Digital Experience
buying_committee.message: Direct owner of autozone.com search and digital UX. Feels the search pain daily. Most likely champion and day-to-day decision driver for search platform selection.

buying_committee.name: Jamere Jackson
buying_committee.title: EVP & CFO
buying_committee.message: Controls technology budget approvals. ROI and payback period language required. Moderate scenario ($36.9M uplift, 3-month payback) is the CFO entry point.

strategic_angles.label: NAPA SAP EOL Moment
strategic_angles.hook: NAPA Auto Parts — the best-performing competitor by engagement — faces a forced SAP Hybris platform migration by July 2026, creating a modernization window for AutoZone to establish an insurmountable search advantage.
pain_points: NAPA has the best engagement in the competitive set (38.4% bounce, 4.44 pages/visit) — but it is loyalty-driven, not search-driven. If they modernize to AI search during re-platform, that gap closes.
AutoZone is the traffic leader with 31.3M monthly visits vs. NAPA's 6.3M — but search quality determines whether that traffic advantage translates to conversion.
Zero of the top 5 competitors use Algolia. First-mover advantage in AI-powered auto parts search is fully available right now.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: NAPA is mid-platform migration right now. If they land on a modern search stack as part of that migration, they close the engagement gap. What's AutoZone's timeline for search modernization — is it ahead of NAPA's migration completion?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia Intelligent Auto Parts Solution launched August 2025 — purpose-built fitment-aware search for auto parts retail. No competitor has deployed it.

strategic_angles.label: Oracle Endeca & Certona Vacuum
strategic_angles.hook: AutoZone has removed Oracle Endeca (search), Oracle ATG (commerce), and Certona (recommendations) simultaneously — three major platform vacuums with no confirmed active replacement in any slot.
pain_points: Oracle Endeca REMOVED — the primary search engine is gone. No confirmed successor in SimilarWeb Feb 2026 technology scan.
Certona recommendations engine REMOVED — no FBT, no 'Customers Also Viewed'. At $18.9B revenue with 100K+ SKUs per Mega Hub, the cross-sell layer is empty.
React/Next.js/Contentstack architecture is headless and API-first — Algolia integrates natively in days, not months.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: After migrating off Oracle Endeca and Certona — what is powering search and recommendations today? Is there a vendor evaluation in progress or is the team building custom?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia InstantSearch React + Algolia Recommend: direct replacement for both Endeca and Certona in a single API-managed platform. Mister-Auto: 12% conversion lift on 1.5M auto parts SKUs.

strategic_angles.label: DIY Natural Language Gap
strategic_angles.hook: 31.3M monthly visitors — including 316K from ChatGPT — arrive with natural language automotive queries that AutoZone's keyword-only search engine cannot process, creating a massive conversion gap at the highest-traffic automotive site in the world.
pain_points: 316,302 monthly visits from ChatGPT — AutoZone's #3 referral source. These visitors arrive with conversational queries ('battery for 2019 Ram 1500') that current NLP-blind search cannot handle.
Headlamp vs. headlight: a 23x synonym gap means 95.7% of headlamp inventory is invisible to customers who use that term.
Typo tolerance failures allow misspelled queries to bypass brand redirects — matching the 49K monthly Google-corrected typo clicks arriving at AutoZone.com.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your VIN Decoder page appears in Google's AI Overview — AutoZone is winning AI-driven discovery. When those AI-referred visitors search on AutoZone.com with conversational queries, how does current search handle them?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia NeuralSearch: 30% more relevant results on natural language queries. Handles headlamp/headlight synonyms, typos, and conversational automotive queries natively.

strategic_angles.label: Commercial (DIFM) B2B Search
strategic_angles.hook: AutoZone's commercial DIFM segment is growing at +9.8% SSS — 6.5x faster than DIY — but the B2B catalog search experience is unoptimized for professional mechanics ordering under time pressure.
pain_points: DIFM commercial at +9.8% SSS is the highest-velocity revenue segment. A mechanic who can't find the right part in under 30 seconds orders from O'Reilly or NAPA — lost share-of-wallet.
AutoZone holds only ~5% commercial market share — 95% of the addressable commercial market is untapped. Search quality determines reorder frequency and share-of-wallet retention.

AutoZonePro and B2B PunchOut catalog (TradeCentric) need account-specific pricing in SAYT, OE part number exact match, and commercial-grade speed — capabilities that require a purpose-built B2B search layer.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your commercial segment grew nearly 10x faster than DIY in Q2 FY2026. Is the catalog search experience specifically optimized for a professional mechanic ordering parts under shop time pressure — or do commercial customers use the same consumer search infrastructure?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia B2B Search: account-aware catalog with commercial-grade speed, part number exact match, and vehicle fitment filtering. Grainger (B2B industrial analog) deployed Algolia for 1M+ SKU commercial catalog.

ae_fields.next_step_action: Send Jamey Maki (VP eCommerce) a 3-bullet personalized observation: 'headlamp' 23-result gap, Duralast brand returning 2 results, ChatGPT as #3 referral source with NLP-blind search. No deck. No pricing.
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: AutoZone's CEO told investors in March 2026 that they are 'investing in technology to build a differentiated and superior customer experience.' We reviewed autozone.com and found five specific places where that investment hasn't reached search yet — including a 23x synonym gap on a core product category and zero recommendations after Certona was removed.
ae_fields.talk_track_cta: Can we get 20 minutes with Jamey Maki and one of your search engineers to walk through the five findings with a live Algolia demo on your own catalog queries?

next_steps.title: Connect with Jamey Maki (VP eCommerce)
next_steps.description: Send a 3-bullet personalized LinkedIn message: headlamp 23x gap, Duralast 2-result brand failure, ChatGPT NLP gap. No deck, no pricing. Reference the Q2 FY2026 catalog expansion quote as urgency context.

next_steps.title: Technical Demo — AutoZone-Specific Queries
next_steps.description: Demo framed around the 5 Phase 2 gaps: headlamp/headlight synonyms, Duralast brand intent, NLP query on 2019 Camry oil, federated 'return policy' search, and empty-state query suggestions. Show Algolia on AutoZone's actual query patterns.

next_steps.title: CIO Architecture Meeting with Michelle Borninkhof
next_steps.description: Architecture overview: API-first headless integration with existing React/Next.js/Contentstack stack. Reference McDonald's/Walmart Algolia-scale familiarity. Supply Chain 2030 completion as the transition hook.

next_steps.title: CFO ROI Presentation for Jamere Jackson
next_steps.description: Quantified model: $36.9M moderate annual revenue impact at ~3-month payback. Dual vector: revenue uplift + CAC reduction from reduced paid search dependency. Three analyst upgrades citing technology as growth catalyst.

next_steps.title: Commercial Pilot — DIFM B2B Catalog
next_steps.description: Propose a focused pilot on the AutoZonePro commercial catalog search. DIFM growing at +9.8% SSS — fastest-path ROI proof point. Part number exact match + account-aware pricing + fitment filtering.

methodology: Phase 1: Pre-Audit Research (14 steps) — Company context via Yahoo Finance MCP (ticker AZO); technology stack via BuiltWith (7 endpoints) and SimilarWeb Technologies API; traffic via SimilarWeb (14 endpoints, Oct 2025–Jan 2026, country: ww); competitor identification and analysis via SimilarWeb Similar Sites + Keyword Competitors + per-competitor traffic and engagement; financial profile via Yahoo Finance MCP (income_stmt, quarterly_income_stmt, balance_sheet, recommendations); investor intelligence via WebFetch of Motley Fool Q2 FY2026 earnings call transcript and Globe and Mail mirror, plus AutoZone 10-K FY2025 SEC filing. Phase 2: Browser-Based Audit — Direct web research and search result analysis for autozone.com (February 2026). 12 core audit steps + 8 Algolia value-prop tests. Screenshot capture at 13 key interaction points. Phase 3: Scoring — Weighted average formula with HIGH severity = 2× weight. Overall score: 2.6/10. All data points tagged [FACT] (API-sourced, reproducible) or [ESTIMATE] (derived, directional).
